definition.name: face_of_convex_set
definition.definition: Let K be a convex set. A face of K is a convex subset F ⊆ K such that whenever x = λy + (1-λ)z with y, z ∈ K and 0 < λ < 1, then x ∈ F implies y, z ∈ F. Equivalently, F is a convex subset of K that "absorbs" any line segment in K passing through its interior. The face generated by a point x ∈ K, denoted F_K(x), is the smallest face of K containing x. It equals the union of all open line segments in K containing x: F_K(x) = {y ∈ K : ∃z ∈ K, ∃λ > 1 such that x = λy + (1-λ)z}. A point x ∈ K is an extreme point of K if and only if F_K(x) = {x}, i.e., dim(F_K(x)) = 0. Note: the face generated by a point always exists (intersection of all faces containing x) but need not be exposed (i.e., need not equal K ∩ H for any supporting hyperplane H). References: Alfsen, Compact Convex Sets and Boundary Integrals, §I.1; Weis-Shirokov, J. Convex Anal. 28(3), 2021, §2.
